compiled_state.author: RebarBotNava
compiled_state.description:
## Why
Benign-vs-competing HEAD-drift tolerance is only tested by calling the `_drift_is_benign`
classifier directly and by two source-inspecting tests: `test_drift_is_benign_helper_exists`
(pure `hasattr`, VALIDATED redundant — siblings already call the helper) and
`test_apply_batch_uses_drift_is_benign` (`inspect.getsource` substring wiring check, which
self-describes as structural). The real `_apply_batch` **continue** path
(`src/rebar/rebar_reconciler/.../applier.py:556,674,718`) is never driven with an injected
drift commit. `tests/.../apply/test_applier_drift.py` drives only the raise path.

## What
Add batch-level tests against a real tickets-branch git fixture that inject a HEAD change
between mutations, and remove the two structural tests.

## Scope
- `tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/mutate/test_apply_batch_drift_tolerance.py`.
- Keep the direct classifier tests (`test_drift_tolerates_*` / `test_drift_rejects_*`).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `test_drift_is_benign_helper_exists` and `test_apply_batch_uses_drift_is_benign`
      are deleted.
- [ ] New behavioral tests cover: benign unrelated-ticket commit continues; same-ticket
      `ticket:` EDIT; `ticket:` DELETE of the targeted ticket; several sequential benign
      commits; a competing `pass_record:` commit aborts before the next Jira call;
      subject-lookup failure treated as non-benign; manifest/`mutations_completed` count
      correct after a mid-pass abort.
- [ ] `make test` green.

file_impact.reason: delete 2 structural tests; add real batch-level drift behavioral tests

compiled_state.title: Real _apply_batch HEAD-drift behavioral tests; delete the two structural drift tests
